manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: grass, sock, satellite, ketchup, code, pyramid, sink, police, boot.
Your opponent's team words are: moscow, capital, pound, tap, phoenix, ruler, saturn, deck.
Distractor words are: field, fish, germany, eye, whale, duck, shoe.
The kill words are: jam.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: grass, sock, satellite, ketchup, code, pyramid, sink, police, boot.
Your opponent's team words are: moscow, capital, pound, tap, phoenix, ruler, saturn, deck.
Distractor words are: field, fish, germany, eye, whale, duck, shoe.
The kill words are: jam.

CLUE: COMPUTER
TARGETS: boot, code, satellite

The words code, satellite were guessed correctly. The word tap was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: grass, sock, ketchup, pyramid, sink, police, boot.
Remaining words for your opponent are: moscow, capital, pound, phoenix, ruler, saturn, deck.
Remaining distractor words are: field, fish, germany, eye, whale, duck, shoe.
Remaining kill words are: jam.

"ACTUALLY", let me reconsider the "parts of them" rule. "The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them."

Does this mean:
1. The clue cannot be a word that appears in the lists (target, opponent, distractor, kill)
2. The clue cannot CONTAIN any of the words in the lists as substrings?

Interpretation 2 would be very restrictive. For example, if "shoe" is in the list, I couldn't use "shoelace" because it contains "shoe". If "tap" is in the list, I couldn't use "tape". If "jam" is in the list, I couldn't use "jamboree".

I think the more reasonable interpretation is that the clue cannot be one of the words, and cannot be a substring of one of the words (i.e., you can't clue "boot" with "boots"). But you could use "copper" even though it contains "cop" because "cop" is not a word in the list. The rule says "contain parts of them" where "them" refers to "the words in the lists". So the clue cannot contain a part that is one of the words in the list.

So "copper" contains "cop". Is "cop" in the list? No, "police" is in the list. "cop" is not. So "copper" should be allowed.

"Shoelace" contains "shoe". "shoe" IS in the list (distractor). So I cannot use "shoelace".

"Tape" contains "tap". "tap" IS in the list (opponent). So I cannot use "tape".

"Jamboree" contains "jam". "jam" IS in the list (kill). So I cannot use "jamboree".

So the rule is: The clue cannot be one of the listed words, and cannot contain any of the listed words as a substring.

"Basin" is fine. It doesn't contain any listed word.
"Steel" is fine. It doesn't contain any listed word.
"Copper" is fine (if I wanted to use it). It doesn't contain any listed word.

"ACTUALLY", let me reconsider the "parts of them" rule. "The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them."

Does this mean:
1. The clue cannot be a word that appears in the lists (target, opponent, distractor, kill)
2. The clue cannot CONTAIN any of the words in the lists as substrings?

Interpretation 2 would be very restrictive. For example, if "shoe" is in the list, I couldn't use "shoelace" because it contains "shoe". If "tap" is in the list, I couldn't use "tape". If "jam" is in the list, I couldn't use "jamboree".

I think the more reasonable interpretation is that the clue cannot be one of the words, and cannot be a substring of one of the words (i.e., you can't clue "boot" with "boots"). But you could use "copper" even though it contains "cop" because "cop" is not a word in the list. The rule says "contain parts of them" where "them" refers to "the words in the lists". So the clue cannot contain a part that is one of the words in the list.

So "copper" contains "cop". Is "cop" in the list? No, "police" is in the list. "cop" is not. So "copper" should be allowed.

"Shoelace" contains "shoe". "shoe" IS in the list (distractor). So I cannot use "shoelace".

"Tape" contains "tap". "tap" IS in the list (opponent). So I cannot use "tape".

"Jamboree" contains "jam". "jam" IS in the list (kill). So I cannot use "jamboree".

So the rule is: The clue cannot be one of the listed words, and cannot contain any of the listed words as a substring.

"Basin" is fine. It doesn't contain any listed word.
"Steel" is fine. It doesn't contain any listed word.
"Copper" is fine (if I wanted to use it). It doesn't contain any listed word.
